title: Delivery OS — Canonical Config & Secret Registry (I-Config, RS-DOS §57.2)
description: THE ONE canonical registry schema for config & secret READINESS. It records METADATA ONLY — never a value (§57.2). It SUPERSEDES both legacy shapes: (a) the admin/PLOS JSON registry {name, owner-as-provider, rule, required, example, fix}, and (b) the PLOS Zod env schema (packages/config/src/env.ts) — every distinction either expresses is carried here. Each entry binds to the §54 data-classification + trust-boundary model: an entry whose data_class is PII or SECRET is a 'secret' and is constrained by the §54.2 trust boundary (sourced/used only within a trusted domain; NEVER tree-resident — §30). The I-Config oracle (i-config.mjs) reads this and returns, per key, PRESENT | MISSING | INVALID | DRIFTED over the live provider planes (§57.4). The oracle NEVER reads or prints a value for a remote-plane key, and never emits a secret value for any key.

schema_version.description: Discriminates the canonical schema from the legacy config-registry.json shape. The oracle reads either; this field marks the canonical one.

environments.description: The §57.2 / §57.4 environment vocabulary this service declares config for. Canonical = {local, dev, QA, staging, prod}. (Legacy 'development'→'dev', 'production'→'prod' are normalized by the oracle when reading a legacy registry.)

planes.description: Where the I-Config oracle reads LIVE state from (the provider planes, §57.4). All ids are read from the environment at run time (never committed). Metadata only — the oracle reads PRESENCE on remote planes, never a value.

orgIdEnv.description: Env var holding the Vercel team/org id (default VERCEL_ORG_ID).

projectIdEnv.description: Env var holding the Vercel project id (default VERCEL_PROJECT_ID).

tokenEnv.description: Env var holding the read-only Vercel token (default VERCEL_TOKEN).

repo.description: owner/repo slug for `gh secret list` presence checks (names only — never values).

key.description: The env var name, exactly as the code reads it. (Supersedes the legacy 'name'.)

owner.description: The ACCOUNTABLE owner of the value — a human/team/role (e.g. 'platform', 'founder', 'data'). §57.2 separates the accountable owner from the source plane. (The legacy registry overloaded 'owner' to mean the provider; here that is 'source_provider'.)

source_provider.description: The PLANE the value lives on / is sourced from (§57.2 source_provider ∈ {vercel|github|supabase|local|…}). 'vercel' = a Vercel project env var; 'github' = a GitHub Actions secret; 'supabase' = sourced from the Supabase dashboard and installed as a Vercel env var (checked on the Vercel plane); 'local' = a local .env (inside the trust boundary). Open set per spec; the oracle's plane readers cover these four today.

data_class.description: The §54.1 classification carrier. data_class ∈ {PII, SECRET} ⇒ this entry is a SECRET and binds to the §54.2 trust boundary (trusted-domain only; NEVER tree-resident — §30). The oracle uses this to decide presence-only handling and to surface trust-boundary obligations; it NEVER weakens to read/print a SECRET/PII value.

env_scope.description: The subset of environments this key APPLIES to (§57.2 env_scope ⊆ {local,dev,QA,staging,prod}). A key present on a plane for an env NOT in its env_scope is DRIFTED (config present where the registry does not scope it).

validation_rule.description: Validation rule id understood by the oracle: postgres-pooler-6543 | postgres-url | url | non-empty | flag | secret-min:N | enum:a|b|c | number | int-nonneg. (Supersedes the legacy 'rule'. The Zod equivalents — .url(), .min(N), .enum(...), coerce.number — map onto these ids; see the adoption plan.)

required_per_env.description: Per-environment requiredness (§57.2 required_per_env). Keys are environment names from env_scope; values are booleans. A key required for an env it is not scoped to is a registry error. (Supersedes the legacy 'required' object.)

purpose.description: What the key is for, in one line. Carried from the legacy registry; preserves the rich PLOS Zod doc-comments when those are migrated.

example.description: A REDACTED example value — NEVER a real secret. For data_class ∈ {PII, SECRET} use a shape placeholder only (e.g. '<32+ char secret>').

fix.description: The exact, actionable remediation the oracle prints when this key is MISSING/INVALID/DRIFTED.

default.description: OPTIONAL — the deterministic default an unset OPTIONAL key falls back to (mirrors a Zod .default(...)). Recorded as metadata so the registry is a faithful superset of the Zod schema; the oracle does not apply it (it reports readiness, it does not load config).

retrieval_source.description: OPTIONAL (Secret Resolver / Vault — RETRIEVAL metadata, distinct from source_provider which is the CONSUMPTION plane). Where the VALUE ORIGINATES when SEEDING the local encrypted vault (the source of truth): 'supabase' = read once from the Supabase origin (dashboard / Management API); 'generated' = minted locally at seed time (e.g. CRON_SECRET = random); 'manual' = pasted once from its origin (the honest default for a write-only origin like a Vercel Sensitive var or a Supabase JWT secret that cannot be read back); 'vault' = already lives only in the vault. Absent ⇒ 'manual'. i-config ignores this field (presence-only); the resolver reads it to know how to seed. NOTE: platforms (Vercel Sensitive env, GitHub Actions secrets) are WRITE-ONLY and are NEVER a retrieval source.

sensitive.description: OPTIONAL. TRUE ⇒ this key is stored on its plane as a WRITE-ONLY value (a Vercel 'Sensitive' env var, or a GitHub Actions secret) whose value the platform API will NEVER return. The resolver treats such a plane as PUSH-only / presence-only and refuses to 'pull' it — the vault is the only read source. Defaults to false.

consumers.description: OPTIONAL. Additional consuming planes the vault's value is PUSHED to (beyond source_provider). Used by the resolver's push() to (re)provision write-only planes. Metadata only.

supabase_project_ref.description: OPTIONAL. The Supabase project ref for a retrieval_source:'supabase' key (used by a future/optional Supabase Management API seed path). Non-secret identifier.
